response:
To find the characteristic polynomial of the given matrix $A$, we need to compute the determinant of the matrix $A - \lambda I$, where $\lambda$ is a scalar variable and $I$ is the $4 \times 4$ identity matrix.

The matrix $A$ is:
$$
A = \left(\begin{array}{cccc}
 \frac{1}{2} & -\frac{101}{8} & -\frac{17}{8} & -\frac{47}{4} \\
 -\frac{75}{8} & -\frac{109}{8} & \frac{111}{8} & -\frac{9}{4} \\
 \frac{27}{8} & \frac{19}{8} & -\frac{21}{2} & -\frac{3}{4} \\
 \frac{35}{4} & \frac{13}{8} & -\frac{21}{2} & \frac{25}{8}
\end{array}\right)
$$

The characteristic polynomial is given by $P(\lambda) = \det(A - \lambda I)$.
$$
A - \lambda I = \left(\begin{array}{cccc}
 \frac{1}{2} - \lambda & -\frac{101}{8} & -\frac{17}{8} & -\frac{47}{4} \\
 -\frac{75}{8} & -\frac{109}{8} - \lambda & \frac{111}{8} & -\frac{9}{4} \\
 \frac{27}{8} & \frac{19}{8} & -\frac{21}{2} - \lambda & -\frac{3}{4} \\
 \frac{35}{4} & \frac{13}{8} & -\frac{21}{2} & \frac{25}{8} - \lambda
\end{array}\right)
$$

Calculating the determinant of a $4 \times 4$ matrix with fractional entries directly is prone to arithmetic errors. A more efficient approach is to check if the matrix has integer eigenvalues or if the characteristic polynomial factors nicely over the integers. Let's try to
